shared_facts: Two US service members were killed in an Iranian missile and drone attack in Jordan on Friday.
One US service member is reported missing following the attack in Jordan.
Four US service members were medically evacuated to Jordanian hospitals and have since been discharged.
Other US personnel evaluated for minor injuries have returned to duty.
latest_clusters.synthesis: US Central Command confirmed that two US service members were killed and one is missing following Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks in Jordan on Friday. Separately, reports indicate ongoing hostilities involving Israeli strikes in Gaza and a series of US strikes against Iranian infrastructure. Regional tensions persist as military engagements continue across multiple fronts.
latest_clusters.confidence: HIGH. The primary report regarding US casualties in Jordan is corroborated by multiple official US military statements cited by diverse sources.

shared_facts: Smoke from Canadian wildfires has drifted into the United States, impacting air quality across multiple regions.
President Donald Trump has expressed dissatisfaction with Canadian forest management regarding these fires.
Air quality alerts have been issued in the United States due to the presence of wildfire smoke.
latest_clusters.synthesis: Canadian wildfire smoke has crossed the border into the United States, causing air quality alerts across several regions and affecting sporting event logistics. President Donald Trump has publicly criticized Canadian forest management and threatened to impose tariffs on Canadian imports in response to the conditions. Regional officials continue to monitor active fires as weather conditions remain a variable in air quality recovery.

US President Donald Trump has issued public statements linking the smoke to Canada and suggesting financial penalties or tariffs.

shared_facts: Jens Spahn has resigned from his position as a parliamentary group leader of the CDU/CSU.
Jens Spahn and his husband, Daniel Funke, used a surrogate mother in the United States to have a child.
Surrogacy is currently banned within Germany.
Jens Spahn was previously critical of surrogacy practices.
latest_clusters.synthesis: Jens Spahn has resigned from his parliamentary leadership role after it was revealed he and his husband used a surrogate in the United States to become parents. The practice of surrogacy remains illegal in Germany and conflicts with the past public statements made by Spahn and the official position of his party. His departure follows internal party pressure regarding the perceived inconsistency between his personal actions and his political record.
latest_clusters.confidence: MEDIUM. There is a factual discrepancy regarding the exact title of the role from which the subject resigned, indicating inconsistent reporting across sources.
